Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Please add a test case.  Also, with this patch new objects can not be created in directories that have a default pool which no longer exists.  We could add a check to lod_ah_init():

                  if (lp->ldo_def_striping_set) {
 -                       if (lp->ldo_pool)
 +                       if (lp->ldo_pool && lod_find_pool(d, lp->ldo_pool))
                                lod_object_set_pool(lc, lp->ldo_pool);

parents.subject: LU-5365 tests: add check_poolname test program

commit.subject: LU-5365 lod: return -ENXIO if an OST pool does not exist
commit.message:
LU-5365 lod: return -ENXIO if an OST pool does not exist

In the current implementation on MDS, an invalid OST pool name
can be accepted while setting striping information on a file
via ioctl() or fsetxattr() interfaces directly. This patch fixes
the codes which handle the return value of lod_find_pool() to
return an error (-ENXIO) if the OST pool does not exist.

Test-Parameters: alwaysuploadlogs \
envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes \
testlist=ost-pools

Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I58d20d4e11fc7ade2189bb13542f9a2591f7ccc9
